{
  "extract_plan": {
    "section_map": [
      {"heading": "Introduction", "location": "pp. 1-5"},
      {"heading": "I. Discovery Phase", "location": "pp. 6-12"},
      {"heading": "II. Initial Acquisition Phase", "location": "pp. 13-17"},
      {"heading": "III. Repeated Acquisition Phase", "location": "pp. 18-23"},
      {"heading": "Appendix A—Compendium of Questions Across All Three Phases", "location": "pp. 24-26"},
      {"heading": "Appendix B—Data Dictionary Template", "location": "p. 27"},
      {"heading": "Footnotes (references to national statistical frameworks)", "location": "pp. 2-3"}
    ],
    "concept_inventory": [
      "administrative data (vs. survey data)",
      "data quality as fitness for use",
      "Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) / data sharing",
      "data file extract",
      "data confidentiality / PII",
      "multi-dimensional structure of data quality",
      "six quality dimensions: Relevance, Accessibility, Coherence, Interpretability, Accuracy, Institutional Environment",
      "Timeliness (subsumed under Relevance)",
      "Comparability (subsumed under Coherence)",
      "three phases: Discovery, Initial Acquisition, Repeated Acquisition",
      "data dictionary",
      "reference time period",
      "program agency vs statistical agency",
      "disclosure control methods",
      "item nonresponse / missing values",
      "duplicate records",
      "data architecture",
      "classification systems / recoding",
      "payment error rate, participation rate (example metrics)",
      "keying and coding errors",
      "SNAP (illustrative example program)",
      "national statistical quality frameworks (ABS, ONS, StatCan, Eurostat)",
      "external standards: USPS ZIP, FIPS, NAICS, OMB race/ethnicity"
    ]
  },
  "concepts": [
    {"id": "co_admin_data", "name": "administrative data", "aliases": ["administrative records"], "description": "Data collected as part of a program agency's routine operations rather than for statistical purposes.", "grounding_span": "which are collected for statistical purposes, administrative data are collected as part of a program", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "co_survey_data", "name": "survey data", "aliases": [], "description": "Data collected for statistical purposes, contrasted with administrative data.", "grounding_span": "Unlike survey data", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "co_data_quality", "name": "data quality", "aliases": ["fitness for use"], "description": "The fitness of data for a use; not homogenous but multi-dimensional.", "grounding_span": "issues of data quality become especially salient", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "co_mou", "name": "Memorandum of Understanding", "aliases": ["MOU"], "description": "Agreement through which a program agency shares administrative records with a statistical agency.", "grounding_span": "Through a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) a program agency may", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "co_extract", "name": "data file extract", "aliases": [], "description": "A file derived or taken from an agency's administrative data files and sent to the receiving statistical agency.", "grounding_span": "a data file extract of those records", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "co_data_sharing", "name": "data sharing", "aliases": ["data-sharing"], "description": "The sharing of administrative records between program and statistical agencies, impeded by various barriers.", "grounding_span": "data-sharing", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "co_confidentiality", "name": "data confidentiality", "aliases": [], "description": "Provisions protecting the confidentiality of shared administrative data.", "grounding_span": "provisions that protect data confidentiality", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "co_relevance", "name": "Relevance", "aliases": [], "description": "Quality dimension: how well the administrative data file meets the needs of the user.", "grounding_span": "Relevance refers to how well the administrative data file meets the needs of the", "location": "Discovery Phase"},
    {"id": "co_accessibility", "name": "Accessibility", "aliases": [], "description": "Quality dimension: ease with which the data file extract can be obtained.", "grounding_span": "Accessibility refers to the ease with which the data file extract can be", "location": "Discovery Phase"},
    {"id": "co_coherence", "name": "Coherence", "aliases": [], "description": "Quality dimension: comparability with other sources and consistency over time and geography.", "grounding_span": "Coherence refers to the degree to which the administrative data are comparable with", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "co_interpretability", "name": "Interpretability", "aliases": [], "description": "Quality dimension: clarity of information ensuring appropriate use of the data.", "grounding_span": "Interpretability refers to the clarity of information to ensure that the", "location": "Discovery Phase"},
    {"id": "co_accuracy", "name": "Accuracy", "aliases": [], "description": "Quality dimension: closeness of administrative record values to their unknown true values.", "grounding_span": "Accuracy refers to the closeness of the administrative record data values to their", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "co_inst_env", "name": "Institutional Environment", "aliases": [], "description": "Quality dimension: credibility of the administrative agency for producing high quality data.", "grounding_span": "Institutional Environment refers to the credibility of the", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "co_timeliness", "name": "Timeliness", "aliases": [], "description": "A dimension in prior frameworks, covered here under Relevance.", "grounding_span": "Timeliness is a common separate dimension in many of the prior frameworks but it is", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "co_comparability", "name": "Comparability", "aliases": [], "description": "A dimension in prior frameworks, covered here under Coherence.", "grounding_span": "Coherence and Comparability are frequently", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "co_discovery", "name": "Discovery phase", "aliases": [], "description": "First phase; covers information needed to approve development of an MOU.", "grounding_span": "Questions in the Discovery phase cover information needed to approve the development of an MOU.", "location": "Discovery Phase"},
    {"id": "co_initial", "name": "Initial Acquisition phase", "aliases": [], "description": "Second phase; from approval to develop the MOU through first-time receipt of the data.", "grounding_span": "The Initial Acquisition phase begins with the approval for developing the MOU, includes the finalizing", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "co_repeated", "name": "Repeated Acquisition phase", "aliases": [], "description": "Third phase; covers periodic receipt of data.", "grounding_span": "Questions in the Repeated Acquisition phase cover periodic receipt of data.", "location": "Repeated Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "co_data_dictionary", "name": "data dictionary", "aliases": [], "description": "Core set of information about the fields of a data file; template provided in Appendix B.", "grounding_span": "typically contained in a data dictionary", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "co_reference_period", "name": "reference time period", "aliases": [], "description": "The time period by which the administrative data are organized.", "grounding_span": "by which the data are organized", "location": "Discovery Phase"},
    {"id": "co_program_agency", "name": "program agency", "aliases": [], "description": "Agency that maintains administrative data for running a government program.", "grounding_span": "the program agency", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "co_statistical_agency", "name": "statistical agency", "aliases": [], "description": "Agency that receives administrative data for statistical purposes.", "grounding_span": "the statistical agency", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "co_pii", "name": "Personally Identifiable Information", "aliases": ["PII"], "description": "Information that must be protected with access limited to authorized persons.", "grounding_span": "Personally Identifiable Information (PII) will be protected", "location": "Discovery Phase"},
    {"id": "co_disclosure_control", "name": "disclosure control methods", "aliases": ["topcoding"], "description": "Modifications of the data file used to protect confidentiality.", "grounding_span": "disclosure control methods", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "co_missing_values", "name": "missing values", "aliases": ["item nonresponse"], "description": "Proportion of missing values for each field; a source of error.", "grounding_span": "proportion of missing values for each field", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "co_duplicate_records", "name": "duplicate records", "aliases": [], "description": "Proportion of duplicate records on the data file extract.", "grounding_span": "duplicate records", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "co_data_architecture", "name": "data architecture", "aliases": [], "description": "The structure and relationship between key variables in the stored data.", "grounding_span": "data architecture and relationship between key variables", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "co_classification_system", "name": "classification system", "aliases": [], "description": "Systems used for categorizing or classifying data (e.g., race, industry).", "grounding_span": "classification systems used for categorizing or classifying the data", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "co_recoding", "name": "recoding", "aliases": [], "description": "Methodology used to recode original data into a value for a new variable.", "grounding_span": "recode original data to create a value for a new variable", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "co_quality_dimension", "name": "quality dimension", "aliases": ["characteristics", "features"], "description": "The recognition that data quality is not homogenous but has several dimensions.", "grounding_span": "not homogenous but instead has several dimensions", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "co_keying_errors", "name": "keying and coding errors", "aliases": [], "description": "Known sources of error in administrative data.", "grounding_span": "keying, coding, and duplication errors", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "co_snap", "name": "Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program", "aliases": ["SNAP", "Food Stamp Program"], "description": "Illustrative program used for the hypothetical example answers.", "grounding_span": "Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program", "location": "Introduction"}
  ],
  "definitions": [
    {"id": "d1", "term": "data quality", "verbatim_text": "Broadly defined, data quality means \"fitness for use.\"", "normative_status": "standard", "as_of_date": "2013-02", "grounding_span": "Broadly defined, data quality means", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "d2", "term": "Relevance", "verbatim_text": "Relevance refers to how well the administrative data file meets the needs of the user in regards to data level (person, family, household, establishment, company, etc.), broad data definitions and concepts, population coverage, time period and timeliness.", "normative_status": "standard", "as_of_date": "2013-02", "grounding_span": "Relevance refers to how well the administrative data file meets the needs of the", "location": "Discovery Phase"},
    {"id": "d3", "term": "Accessibility", "verbatim_text": "Accessibility refers to the ease with which the data file extract can be obtained from the administrative agency. This includes the suitability of the form or medium for transferring the data, confidentiality constraints, and cost.", "normative_status": "standard", "as_of_date": "2013-02", "grounding_span": "Accessibility refers to the ease with which the data file extract can be", "location": "Discovery Phase"},
    {"id": "d4", "term": "Coherence", "verbatim_text": "Coherence refers to the degree to which the administrative data are comparable with other data sources and consistent over time and across geographical areas.", "normative_status": "standard", "as_of_date": "2013-02", "grounding_span": "Coherence refers to the degree to which the administrative data are comparable with", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "d5", "term": "Interpretability", "verbatim_text": "Interpretability refers to the clarity of information to ensure that the administrative data are utilized in an appropriate way. This includes evaluation of data collection forms, data collection instructions, and a data dictionary.", "normative_status": "standard", "as_of_date": "2013-02", "grounding_span": "Interpretability refers to the clarity of information to ensure that the", "location": "Discovery Phase"},
    {"id": "d6", "term": "Accuracy", "verbatim_text": "Accuracy refers to the closeness of the administrative record data values to their (unknown) true values. This includes information on any known sources of errors in the administrative data such as missing records, missing values of individual data items, misinterpretation of questions, and keying, coding, and duplication errors.", "normative_status": "standard", "as_of_date": "2013-02", "grounding_span": "Accuracy refers to the closeness of the administrative record data values to their", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "d7", "term": "Institutional Environment", "verbatim_text": "Institutional Environment refers to the credibility of the administrative agency for producing high quality and reliable administrative data. This includes an evaluation of the agency's quality standards and processes for assuring adherence to standards.", "normative_status": "standard", "as_of_date": "2013-02", "grounding_span": "Institutional Environment refers to the credibility of the", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"}
  ],
  "claims": [
    {"id": "cl1", "claim_text": "Administrative data are collected as part of a program agency's routine operations, unlike survey data collected for statistical purposes.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "which are collected for statistical purposes, administrative data are collected as part of a program", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "cl2", "claim_text": "The extent and effectiveness of data-sharing are impeded by various barriers.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "The extent and effectiveness of data-sharing are impeded by", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "cl3", "claim_text": "One of these barriers is limited information on how to assess the quality of the data.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "One of these barriers is limited information on how to assess the quality of the data.", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "cl4", "claim_text": "Data quality is not homogenous but instead has several dimensions.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "not homogenous but instead has several dimensions", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "cl5", "claim_text": "The multi-dimensional structure is a common feature of the data quality frameworks for other national statistical offices.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "dimensional structure is a common feature of the data quality frameworks for other national statistical", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "cl6", "claim_text": "Using the Tool does not result in a single overall numerical measure for data quality.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "Using the Tool does not result in a single overall numerical measure (or metric or index) for data quality.", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "cl7", "claim_text": "The Tool contains 43 questions organized by three phases of the data sharing process.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "The Tool contains 43 questions organized by three", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "cl8", "claim_text": "The time required for the data provider's staff to complete the Tool for the early phases is predicted to be about six to ten hours if a data dictionary is already available.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "predicted to be about six to ten hours", "location": "Introduction"}
  ],
  "instruments": [
    {"id": "inst_tool", "name": "Data Quality Assessment Tool for Administrative Data", "owner": "Federal Committee on Statistical Methodology (FCSM) Data Quality Working Group", "year": 2013, "method": "43 questions organized by three phases (Discovery, Initial Acquisition, Repeated Acquisition) and six quality dimensions; answers are qualitative and quantitative, yielding no single index.", "grounding_span": "Data Quality Assessment Tool for Administrative Data", "location": "Introduction"}
  ],
  "measures": [
    {"id": "m1", "text": "What are the general contents of the administrative data file(s) maintained by the program?", "response_type": "qualitative", "grounding_span": "What are the general contents of the administrative data file(s) maintained by the program?", "location": "Discovery Phase"},
    {"id": "m2", "text": "What are the earliest and latest dates for which administrative data are available (including in archive)?", "response_type": "qualitative", "grounding_span": "What are the earliest and latest dates for which administrative data are available (including in", "location": "Discovery Phase"},
    {"id": "m3", "text": "How soon after a reference time period ends can a data file extract be prepared and provided?", "response_type": "qualitative", "grounding_span": "How soon after a reference time period ends can a data file extract be prepared and provided?", "location": "Discovery Phase"},
    {"id": "m4", "text": "Describe any legal, regulatory or administrative restrictions on access to the data file extract.", "response_type": "qualitative", "grounding_span": "Describe any legal, regulatory or administrative restrictions on access to the data file extract.", "location": "Discovery Phase"},
    {"id": "m5", "text": "What is the typical reimbursement for sharing your data? Are reports, a data product, or a monetary fee given in return?", "response_type": "qualitative", "grounding_span": "What is the typical reimbursement for sharing your data? Are reports, a data product, or a", "location": "Discovery Phase"},
    {"id": "m6", "text": "How will the data file extract be transferred to the user?", "response_type": "qualitative", "grounding_span": "How will the data file extract be transferred to the user?", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "m7", "text": "Describe each variable on the administrative data file and note their valid values.", "response_type": "qualitative", "grounding_span": "Describe each variable on the administrative data file and note their valid values. (See template", "location": "Discovery Phase"},
    {"id": "m8", "text": "Describe the methodology used to recode original data to create a value for a new variable.", "response_type": "qualitative", "grounding_span": "Describe the methodology used to recode original data to create a value for a new variable,", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "m9", "text": "Describe the data architecture and relationship between key variables.", "response_type": "qualitative", "grounding_span": "Describe the data architecture and relationship between key variables. For example, are data", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "m10", "text": "Please describe any classification systems used for categorizing or classifying the data.", "response_type": "qualitative", "grounding_span": "Please describe any classification systems used for categorizing or classifying the data.", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "m11", "text": "Were there changes during the file extract period to geographical boundaries?", "response_type": "qualitative", "grounding_span": "Were there changes during the file extract period to geographical boundaries?", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "m12", "text": "What investigations/analyses have been conducted that reveal data quality characteristics?", "response_type": "qualitative", "grounding_span": "What investigations/analyses have been conducted that reveal data quality characteristics (such", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "m13", "text": "What is the proportion of duplicate records on the data file extract?", "response_type": "quantitative", "grounding_span": "What is the proportion of duplicate records on the data file extract?", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "m14", "text": "What is the proportion of missing values for each field?", "response_type": "quantitative", "grounding_span": "What is the proportion of missing values for each field?", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "m15", "text": "What are the known sources of errors in the administrative data (e.g. non-response, keying, coding errors)?", "response_type": "qualitative", "grounding_span": "What are the known sources of errors in the administrative data (e.g. non-response, keying,", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "m16", "text": "Describe the purpose of the administrative program.", "response_type": "qualitative", "grounding_span": "Describe the purpose of the administrative program.", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "m17", "text": "Describe your processes for data collection, editing, review, correction, dissemination, and retention.", "response_type": "qualitative", "grounding_span": "Describe your processes for data collection, editing, review, correction, dissemination, and", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "m18", "text": "For the file extract period, describe any new records or revisions to existing records that may occur after data acquisition.", "response_type": "qualitative", "grounding_span": "For the file extract period, describe any new records or revisions to existing records that may", "location": "Repeated Acquisition Phase"}
  ],
  "standards": [
    {"id": "std_usps_zip", "name": "USPS ZIP Code Standard", "version": "dated Jan 1, 2005", "steward": "United States Postal Service", "as_of_date": "2005-01-01", "grounding_span": "USPS ZIP Code Standard", "location": "Appendix B"},
    {"id": "std_fips", "name": "FIPS County code standard", "version": "dated Oct 24, 1998", "steward": "Federal Information Processing Standards", "as_of_date": "1998-10-24", "grounding_span": "FIPS County code", "location": "Discovery Phase"},
    {"id": "std_naics", "name": "North American Industry Classification System (NAICS)", "version": "", "steward": "Office of Management and Budget", "as_of_date": "2013-02", "grounding_span": "North American Industry Classification System (NAICS)", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"},
    {"id": "std_omb", "name": "OMB race and ethnicity categories", "version": "", "steward": "Office of Management and Budget", "as_of_date": "2013-02", "grounding_span": "the current race and ethnicity categories defined", "location": "Initial Acquisition Phase"}
  ],
  "frameworks": [
    {"id": "fw_sixdim", "name": "Six-dimension administrative data quality framework", "owner": "FCSM Data Quality Working Group", "year": 2013, "grounding_span": "six quality dimensions", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "fw_abs", "name": "ABS Data Quality Framework", "owner": "Australian Bureau of Statistics", "year": 2009, "grounding_span": "ABS Data Quality Framework, May 2009", "location": "Footnotes"},
    {"id": "fw_ons", "name": "ONS Guidelines for Measuring Statistical Quality", "owner": "United Kingdom's Office of National Statistics", "year": 2013, "grounding_span": "ONS Guidelines for Measuring Statistical Quality", "location": "Footnotes"},

    {"id": "fw_eurostat", "name": "European Statistics Code of Practice", "owner": "Eurostat", "year": 2011, "grounding_span": "European Statistics Code of Practice", "location": "Footnotes"}
  ],

    {"grounding_span": "data quality dimensions of Relevance, Accessibility, and Interpretability are central to the Discovery", "suggested_edge": "dimension_central_to_phase", "note": "Specific quality dimensions are designated as central to a specific phase — a Concept(dimension)→Concept(phase) salience relationship not expressible by the allowed edge types.", "location": "Discovery Phase"},
    {"grounding_span": "two of which are new while nine are repeated from the Discovery or Initial Acquisition phase.", "suggested_edge": "measure_reused_in_phase", "note": "Questions/measures recur across phases; there is no edge type for reuse of a Measure across phase Concepts.", "location": "Repeated Acquisition Phase"},
    {"grounding_span": "The Tool contains 43 questions organized by three", "suggested_edge": "instrument_organized_by", "note": "The Instrument is organized by phase and dimension — a structural organization relationship (Instrument→Concept) beyond operationalizes/measures.", "location": "Introduction"}
